channel.title: Metals Industry News Feed
channel.description: Automated news feed for aluminum, steel, copper, and nickel industries

item.title: Aluminum Shortages Coming Soon || Peter Zeihan
item.description: Peter Zeihan warns that aluminum production in the Persian Gulf is shutting down due to Iranian strikes on gas fields, pipelines, and power plants, affecting smelters reliant on cheap natural gas. Facilities in the region, including some of the world's largest, are operating at less than half capacity and may fully shut down within weeks, accounting for about 9% of global primary aluminum. This shortage, combined with existing US tariffs, will drive up primary aluminum prices.

item.title: Steel rebar futures fall below CNY 3,130 per ton amid Tangshan pollution emergency
item.description: Steel rebar futures hit a two-week low as China's steelmaking hub Tangshan activated emergency pollution response measures, raising concerns about potential output curbs. Chinese steel production declined 3.6% to 76.1 million tons in February, while global output fell 2.2% to 141.8 million tons. Steel prices rose 0.13% to 3,126 CNY/T on March 27, though they remain 2.22% lower than a year ago.

item.title: MOC: Average high-speed wire rod price in China up 0.2% in Mar 16-22, 2026
item.description: Chinese high-speed wire rod prices increased 0.2% during the week of March 16-22, 2026, reflecting modest upward movement in the domestic market.

item.title: Copper - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Copper prices fluctuated around 5.44-5.47 USD per pound on March 27, down slightly daily but poised for a weekly gain amid hopes for a diplomatic resolution to the US-Iran conflict. President Trump extended a deadline for striking Iranian infrastructure to allow negotiations, with Iran permitting oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz. Over the past month, prices fell about 7%, though still higher year-over-year.

item.title: Iranian Steel Plants Damaged: Supply Chain Crisis Unfolds
item.description: Iranian steel plants with significant projected annual capacity have been damaged, creating supply chain disruptions. The incident impacts global steel market dynamics during a period of already-strained capacity.

item.title: Steel Manufacturing Industry Trends 2026 | Smart & Sustainable Steel
item.description: The steel manufacturing industry is experiencing significant transformation driven by technology, sustainability, and global demand shifts. Companies are investing in hydrogen-based steelmaking, scrap steel recycling, and smart manufacturing techniques to reduce emissions, improve efficiency, and maintain competitive advantage.

item.title: Global Iron and Steel Prices and News
item.description: SteelRadar provides comprehensive coverage of global iron and steel industry pricing, news, and analyses. Recent updates include reporting on scrap market upward movement with increased trading activity and cost pressure, as well as industry leadership engagement with young professionals at the 'From Experience to the Future' event.

item.title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Nickel rose to 17,215 USD/T on March 27, 2026, up 0.29% from the previous day, rebounding from a recent over one-month low to around $17,400 per tonne. The increase is driven by hopes for a temporary de-escalation in the Middle East conflict following a US-proposed one-month ceasefire to Iran, boosting risk sentiment. Additionally, Nickel Industries received approval for 14.3 million wet metric tonnes of 2026 nickel ore sales in Indonesia, supporting supply clarity and futures prices.

item.title: Global Iron and Steel Prices and News 30.03.2026
item.description: Hadeed signals upward pressure on Saudi steel pricing for February 2026 amid ongoing market dynamics. Stainless steel futures show flat pricing on 1-, 2-, and 3-month terms as of March 27, 2026. Broader news covers geopolitical impacts like Hormuz closures and attacks on Iranian steel facilities, alongside export awards.

item.title: [SMM Nickel Midday Commentary] On March 30, nickel prices dropped slightly, and US voters’ dissatisfaction with Trump hit a record high.
item.description: On March 30, nickel prices experienced a slight drop according to SMM's midday commentary. The report notes this movement amid broader market observations. US voters' dissatisfaction with Trump reached a record high, though its direct link to nickel pricing remains unclear.

item.title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Aluminum prices rose to 3,431.50 USD/T on March 30, 2026, marking a 4.77% increase from the previous day. Over the past month, prices have risen 7.42%, and are up 35.27% compared to the same time last year. Aluminum futures in the UK held near $3,250 per tonne amid assessments of supply developments and demand prospects.

item.title: Aluminum prices surged 6 percent, nearing a four-year high | Economic Summary March 30, 2026
item.description: Aluminum prices surged 6% on the London Metal Exchange, nearing a four-year high and reaching 3,492 USD per ton for the three-month contract. The increase follows attacks on Middle Eastern smelters and disruptions in shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Geopolitical tensions involving Iran, Israel, and the US have impacted the aluminum business.

item.title: Aluminum prices surged 6 percent, nearing a four-year high
item.description: Aluminum prices on the London Metal Exchange surged 6% on Monday, approaching a four-year high. The increase follows attacks on aluminum smelters in the Middle East and disruptions in shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. This event highlights ongoing supply chain vulnerabilities impacting global aluminum production.

item.title: Aluminum Prices Jump as Iran Hit Middle East Producers
item.description: Aluminum prices increased 5.27% to 3447.88 USD/T on March 30, 2026. The jump is attributed to Iranian strikes impacting Middle East aluminum producers. This contributes to recent market volatility and upward trends.

item.title: SMS group Successfully Modernizes Hyundai Steel's Continuous Galvanizing Line
item.description: SMS group received the final acceptance certificate from Hyundai Steel for modernizing its continuous galvanizing line #1 at the Dangjin plant in Korea. This modernization upgrade enhances the facility's technological capabilities and efficiency.

item.title: EGA and Alba Damaged In Recent Attacks from Iran
item.description: Major aluminum producers EGA and Alba sustained damage from recent attacks originating from Iran. This incident impacts global aluminum supply and production capacity.

item.title: Iran bombs Middle East's largest aluminum plant! 9% of ...
item.description: Iran has bombed the Middle East's largest aluminum plant, potentially affecting 9% of regional production capacity. The attack exacerbates supply disruptions in the aluminum market amid escalating geopolitical tensions. This incident contributes to heightened volatility in global aluminum supply chains.

item.title: Future of Steel: Trends, Technology & Sustainability 2026
item.description: The global steel industry is undergoing radical transformation toward decarbonization, with companies adopting DRI steel, molten oxide electrolysis, and enhanced scrap recycling. These innovations are reshaping the entire steel value chain, with scrap-based production, direct reduced iron technology, and green steel methods driving competitiveness in 2026.

item.title: Innovations in Sustainable Steel
item.description: The steel industry is at a critical juncture for reducing emissions and enhancing sustainability through technologies such as scrap-based electric arc furnaces (EAF) and hydrogen-based solutions.

item.title: Stainless steel manufacturing in 2026: the innovations and market forces reshaping the industry
item.description: Demand in stainless steel is shifting from commodity-grade applications in conventional construction toward technology-intensive end uses, including new energy systems.

item.title: Nickel Prices Experienced Wide Fluctuations and Declined in March
item.description: Nickel prices in China experienced volatile fluctuations and declined in March 2026, dropping from 143,216.67 RMB/ton at the month's start to 137,683.33 RMB/ton by the end, a 3.86% decrease. Supply-side factors like delays in Indonesia's approvals and production halts provided cost support, while high inventories and weak demand capped upside. The market shifted from expectation-based trading to facing realities, with steel and battery sectors showing mixed demand.

item.title: Outokumpu sells remaining LP business to Cogne
item.description: Outokumpu has signed an agreement to divest its longs-producing units in Degerfors and Storfors, Sweden, to Italy-based Cogne Acciai Speciali, fully exiting the long products business. Cogne Acciai Speciali views this as the first step in its growth strategy to become a global leader in special long stainless steels. CEO Massimiliano Burelli highlighted the integration of the Outokumpu team and expansion in Nordic and Baltic distribution.

item.title: Steel fell to 3,124 CNY/T on March 31, 2026
item.description: Steel rebar futures declined 0.64% to 3,124 CNY/T as investors assessed supply constraints linked to the Iran war, which have elevated fuel and shipping costs. Israel's strikes on two major Iranian steel factories tied to the IRGC have added further complexity to the global steel market.

item.title: HRC Steel rose to 1,063.94 USD/T on March 31, 2026
item.description: Hot-Rolled Coil Steel increased 0.09% to 1,063.94 USD/T, reaching its highest level since January 2024. Over the past month, HRC Steel gained 4.72%, and is up 18.88% compared to the same period last year.

item.title: US raw steel production increases by 1.2 percent - week 13, 2026
item.description: U.S. raw steel production increased 1.2 percent in the week ending March 28, 2026, reaching 1,803,000 net tons with a capability utilization rate of 77.9 percent. This represents a 5.9 percent increase compared to the same week in the previous year, and year-to-date production through March 28, 2026 was up 5.0 percent from the prior year.

item.title: Copper held steady near $5.5 per pound on Tuesday but remained on track to lose nearly 10% in March
item.description: Copper held steady near $5.5 per pound on Tuesday but is on track for a nearly 10% loss in March, its worst monthly performance since July last year. Industrial metals face pressure from Middle East conflict disruptions and surging energy prices, raising inflation and slowing global activity concerns. Copper rose to 5.55 USD/Lbs on March 31, up 1.30% from the previous day.

item.title: U.S. Steel Resumes Operations on Granite City Works' Blast Furnace
item.description: United States Steel Corporation successfully restarted blast furnace B at Granite City Works in Granite City, Illinois. This operational resumption represents a significant development in U.S. steel production capacity.

item.title: BENTELER Taps SMS group For Stretch-Reducing Mill Upgrade
item.description: SMS group announced it will modernize BENTELER Steel/Tube GmbH & Co. KG's stretch-reducing mill in Paderborn Schloss Neuhaus, Germany. This modernization project will improve the facility's operational efficiency and technological capabilities.

item.title: Ivanhoe stuns market with deep Kamoa-Kakula output cut
item.description: Ivanhoe Mines has revised its plan for the Kamoa-Kakula copper mine complex in the DRC, lowering near-term production volumes to prioritize operational stability and long-term efficiency gains. This significant output cut has surprised the market amid ongoing supply concerns. The adjustment affects copper production forecasts for the region.

item.title: Ivanhoe says revised report cuts 2026 copper output forecast for DRC mine
item.description: Ivanhoe Mines lowered its 2026 copper anode production estimate for the Kamoa-Kakula mine in the DRC to a range of 290,000 to 330,000 tonnes. The revision reflects adjustments to ensure stability in operations. This comes as global copper supply faces various disruptions.

item.title: AMAG and Pilatus Sign Multi-Year Agreement for Aluminum Products in Aircraft
item.description: AMAG and Pilatus have entered into a multi-year agreement for aluminum products in aircraft applications. This partnership represents continued innovation in aerospace aluminum solutions.

item.title: Live Aluminum Prices Today
item.description: Aluminum prices advanced 0.85% today, reaching $3,277.86 per ton globally and ¥22,656 in China, with a later update showing 0.17% rise to $3,425.83/ton globally and ¥23,605/ton in China. The upward momentum stems from geopolitical shockwaves due to Iranian strikes on major Middle East smelters like Emirates Global Aluminium and Aluminium Bahrain, plus the Strait of Hormuz closure, threatening 9% of global primary aluminum supply. This supply bottleneck supports elevated prices amid a structural deficit.

item.title: Nucor increases consumer spot price for hot-rolled coil
item.description: Nucor has raised its consumer spot price for hot-rolled coil to $1,035 per short ton, a $10/st increase from the previous week. Sheet and plate prices have remained flat or modestly higher, continuing a trend observed since the beginning of Q4.

item.title: U.S. Steel confirms restart of Granite City B blast furnace
item.description: U.S. Steel has confirmed the restart of the B blast furnace at its Granite City Works facility near St. Louis, signaling continued capacity expansion efforts.

item.title: Chilean miner Codelco’s El Teniente smelter to restart after collapse
item.description: Chilean miner Codelco’s smelter at its El Teniente mine is set to restart on Thursday after a collapse. This development could impact copper supply from one of the world's largest producers. The restart follows an unspecified incident at the facility.

item.title: USWC docks containerized ferrous prices stable as bulk drops $10/gt
item.description: US West Coast docks reported stable containerized ferrous prices on April 1, while bulk prices dropped $10 per gross ton. This reflects mixed trends in the ferrous scrap market amid fluctuating demand.

item.title: Turkish domestic and export rebar prices surge further amid higher costs
item.description: Turkish rebar prices for both domestic and export markets surged further on April 1 due to elevated production costs, despite subdued demand. This continues a recent upward trend in regional steel pricing.

item.title: Baosteel's Danieli Fastarc Zerobucket EAF Beats Production Record
item.description: Danieli announced that Baosteel's new Fastarc Zerobucket electric arc furnace (EAF) meltshop has achieved a significant operational milestone by reaching 31 heats daily, surpassing the previous 10-year production record. This advancement demonstrates improved efficiency in EAF technology for steel production.

item.title: Copper: Chile output slump and mine cuts – ING
item.description: Chile's copper output has slumped, contributing to global supply concerns, alongside production cuts at various mines. Analysts from ING highlight these developments as key factors pressuring supply. This aligns with recent reports of low production in the country.

item.title: Copper dips as oil rallies after Trump flags more Iran attacks
item.description: Copper prices experienced a dip as oil markets rallied following statements about potential Iran attacks. The article reflects ongoing market volatility driven by geopolitical tensions affecting commodity prices.

item.title: Ipsa Chile up 2.1% on Iran rally, Copper low
item.description: Chilean equity markets rose 2.1% amid Iran-related geopolitical developments, while copper prices remained low. The article highlights the correlation between geopolitical events and commodity market movements.

item.title: Copper: Chile output slump and mine cuts
item.description: Report on declining copper output from Chile, the world's largest mine copper producer, and associated mine production cuts impacting global supply.

item.title: Aluminium Giants Hit Major Milestone with Low-Carbon Production
item.description: Montreal-based Elysis, a joint venture of Alcoa and Rio Tinto, announced a breakthrough in scaling its carbon-free 'inert anode' technology by deploying an industrial-size, carbon-free anode in an existing smelter in Alma, Quebec. This full-scale prototype represents a significant step up from the company's 100-kA pilot unit, which has already produced low-carbon aluminium for Apple products, Michelob Ultra beer cans, and Audi electric vehicle wheels.

item.title: Our roadmap to net-zero emissions in aluminium production
item.description: Hydro announced its commitment to achieve net-zero emissions in aluminium production by 2050 or earlier, with plans to reduce emissions by 30 percent by 2030. The company is developing proprietary HalZero technology that converts alumina to aluminium chloride prior to electrolysis, keeping chlorine and carbon in a closed loop to avoid CO₂ emissions.

item.title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Steel prices rose to 3104 CNY/T on April 2, 2026, up 0.03% from the previous day, amid supply constraints from the Iran war impacting fuel and shipping costs. Steel rebar futures fell toward CNY 3,120 per ton on March 31, 2026, down 0.76%, with Israeli strikes on Iranian steel factories adding market complexity. Over the past month, prices have risen 1.21%, though still 2.24% lower year-over-year.

item.title: Live Copper Prices Today
item.description: Copper prices edged lower today, down 0.17% to $5.63/lb (¥85,395/Ton in China), as initial relief over easing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East began to fade. Macroeconomic uncertainties weighed on market sentiment, with rising stockpiles on LME nearing six-year highs and SHFE stocks near record levels, combined with mixed Chinese manufacturing data tempering speculative bets. Ample near-term supply offset underlying structural support.

item.title: Aluminum fell to 3,468.50 USD/T on April 2, 2026
item.description: Aluminum futures in the UK slipped toward $3,500 per tonne but remained near their highest level in over four years, amid fears of a supply squeeze following geopolitical tensions. President Trump warned of severe military action against Iran over the next two to three weeks, contributing to market uncertainty about future supply disruptions.

item.title: Barrick warns of 'significant increases' to budget, timeline for Pakistan copper project
item.description: Barrick Gold has warned of substantial budget and timeline increases for its Reko Diq copper project in Pakistan's Balochistan province, one of the world's largest undeveloped copper deposits.

item.title: Chinese copper miners join $1.2 billion African rail revamp
item.description: Copper producers CMOC and Zijin are partnering with state-owned CCECC to upgrade a 1,860-km rail line to Dar es Salaam in Tanzania, representing a $1.2 billion investment in African mining infrastructure.

item.title: KGHM mine in Chile reaches early contract agreement with unions
item.description: KGHM's Chilean mining operations reached early labor contract agreements covering more than 1,600 employees across multiple unions, securing labor stability for copper production.

item.title: No sign yet of China’s plan to cut copper output in major smelters’ results
item.description: Major Chinese smelters are planning to raise or maintain copper output in 2026, despite a public commitment by the state-linked industry association to cut production by over 10%. This indicates no immediate signs of the planned reduction materializing. The development comes amid ongoing market pressures on copper supply.

item.title: Barrick warns of “significant increases” to budget, timeline for Pakistan copper project Reko Diq
item.description: Barrick has warned of significant increases to the budget and timeline for the Reko Diq copper project in Pakistan, one of the world’s largest undeveloped copper deposits. The project is located in the remote Balochistan province. These delays could impact future copper production capacity from the site.

item.title: Copper-Aluminum Divergence Unveils The Complex Iran War Impact
item.description: The divergence between copper and aluminum prices highlights the complex impact of the Iran war on metal markets. Copper production and supply chains may face disruptions due to geopolitical tensions. This analysis reveals ongoing risks to global copper output.

item.title: Copper-Aluminum divergence unveils the complex Iran war impact
item.description: Analysis of how the Iran war is creating divergent impacts on copper and aluminum markets, with copper prices being affected by geopolitical tensions and supply chain disruptions.

item.title: MOC: Average high-speed wire rod price in China down 0.3% in Mar 23-29, 2026
item.description: China's average high-speed wire rod price fell 0.3% during March 23-29, 2026, reflecting softening in certain steel segments. This trend contributes to broader concerns over Chinese steel demand. Prices remain under pressure amid various market factors.

item.title: Aluminum Price Analysis Today - Apr 3, 2026
item.description: Aluminum futures retreated by 1.62%, settling at $3,461.28 per ton globally following recent massive price spikes. The pullback reflects selling pressure as markets priced in an anticipated influx of imported units to rebuild depleted inventories, alongside easing procurement anxieties following newly announced U.S. tariff reductions on specific aluminum derivative goods.

item.title: Nickel Price Analysis Today
item.description: Global nickel prices retreated 0.93% to $17,081.3 per ton, driven by persistent structural oversupply and sluggish downstream demand from the Asian stainless steel sector. Elevated LME inventories, rising Chinese social stocks, and a robust US dollar are further pressuring the metal, though Indonesia's mining quota restrictions and proposed export taxes provide a cost floor.

item.title: Nickel Futures Trading Analysis
item.description: Nickel futures are trading around $17,200, falling from March highs near $17,770. Supply remains constrained as Indonesia advances its proposed export tax on nickel, while disruptions to shipping through the Strait of Hormuz add logistical pressure. Trading Economics forecasts nickel to trade at $17,924.26 by the end of Q2 2026.

item.title: US considers revising Section 232 tariffs with lower rates on steel derivatives
item.description: The US is considering revisions to Section 232 tariffs, potentially lowering rates on steel derivatives while keeping others near 50%. This follows reports impacting steel rebar futures and market sentiment. The news contributes to ongoing pressure on steel prices.

item.title: Iran’s Mobarakeh Steel confirms complete shutdown after second US-Israeli strike
item.description: Iran's Mobarakeh Steel has shut down completely following a second US-Israeli strike, disrupting supply chains. This event aligns with mentions of Mideast disruptions affecting steel prices. Global market turbulence is expected from such geopolitical events.

item.title: Cogne Acciai Speciali SpA Announces Its Commitment to Sustainability with ResponsibleSteel™ Certification
item.description: Cogne Acciai Speciali has embarked on the ResponsibleSteel™ certification process for its Aosta site, a global standard promoting sustainability across the steel production value chain. This decision formalizes the company's commitment to environmental, social, and transparency standards as the steel industry faces increasing pressure to meet environmental and social challenges.

item.title: Live Nickel Prices Today
item.description: Global nickel prices remain flat at $17,081.3 per ton, with Chinese markets at ¥117,559 per ton. Downward pressures from oversupply, weak stainless steel demand, and high LME inventories are balanced by Indonesia's production controls, export taxes, and rising costs. This creates a firm price floor amid competing forces.

item.title: Industry Data - Week Ending April 4, 2026
item.description: Domestic raw steel production reached 1,831,000 net tons in the week ending April 4, 2026, with a capability utilization rate of 79.1 percent, representing an 8.2 percent increase from the same period last year. Year-to-date production through April 4, 2026 was 24,028,000 net tons at a 77.4 percent utilization rate, up 5.2 percent from the prior year.

item.title: Codelco sees war disruptions adding 5% to cost of making copper
item.description: Chile's state copper company Codelco reports that ongoing war disruptions are increasing copper production costs by approximately 5%, affecting operational economics.

item.title: Daily Steel Prices
item.description: Daily steel prices updated on April 6, 2026, show weekly changes for scrap grades like HMS I/II 80:20 CFR Turkey up 7.44%, Shredded CFR Turkey up 7.06%, and others ranging from 6.47% to 7.35%. Prices for HDG and PPGI FOB China reflect 2.51% and 2.47% weekly increases. Data provides highest, lowest, and average levels for major global products.

item.title: Panama moves to unlock copper from First Quantum mine
item.description: Panama's Commerce and Industries Minister may approve by Tuesday the exports of already mined ore from Cobre Panama’s stockpiles. This move aims to unlock copper production from First Quantum's operations amid ongoing discussions. It follows a revised plan for the vast copper mine complex that lowers near-term volumes while prioritizing long-term efficiency.

item.title: Live Nickel Prices Today - Nickel Price Analysis Today Apr 6, 2026
item.description: Nickel prices remained flat at $17,081.3/Ton globally and ¥117,559/Ton in China, within a tight trading range. The market is supported by Indonesia’s reduced ore production quotas easing oversupply fears, recovering Chinese stainless steel and EV demand, and declining LME inventories. Upside is capped by tiered Indonesian royalty rates at higher prices.

item.title: World Steel News
item.description: Meranti Green Steel reaffirmed its 2030 production timeline for a high value-added steel project in Duqm, focusing on expanding overall capacity. The update highlights ongoing plans for green steel production amid global industry developments.

item.title: Aluminum fell to 3,464.50 USD/T on April 7, 2026
item.description: Aluminum prices fell 0.12% to $3,464.50 per tonne, though futures in the UK have risen toward $3,500 per tonne, approaching their highest level in more than four years. The price volatility is driven by geopolitical tensions, with Emirates Global Aluminium halting operations at its Al Taweelah smelter after Iranian missile strikes, and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz severely disrupting outbound shipments from the Gulf region, which supplies roughly 9% of global aluminum.

item.title: Japanese copper smelters' H1 output seen rising 3.3% y/y
item.description: Japanese copper smelters are expected to increase their first-half output by 3.3% year-over-year. Pan Pacific Copper, the largest supplier, anticipates its first-half supply to rise 7.5% from the previous year.

item.title: Vale advances Oman maintenance shutdowns to counter war impacts
item.description: Vale has accelerated maintenance shutdowns at its Oman operations, which have an annual production capacity of 9 million tons of iron ore pellets—roughly 29% of the company's overall output last year. The move is intended to counter impacts from geopolitical tensions.

item.title: Nickel Fell to 17,025 USD/T on April 7, 2026
item.description: Nickel fell to 17,025 USD/T on April 7, 2026, down 0.12% from the previous day. Monthly decline stands at 2.32%, though prices are 20.32% above last year. Supply constraints from Indonesia's export tax and Strait of Hormuz disruptions add pressure.

item.title: Aluminium prices surge over 10% as Iran war disrupts global supply
item.description: Aluminium prices are soaring due to supply disruptions across the Gulf region, with key smelters facing shutdowns and exports disrupted via the Strait of Hormuz. LME aluminium futures have surged over 10% since the conflict began, with global inventories hitting multi-month lows and experts warning of a deepening global supply deficit.

item.title: Aluminum Price Analysis Today - April 7, 2026
item.description: Aluminum prices remained flat globally at $3,461.28 per ton, with the primary drivers being Iranian missile strikes that severely damaged major smelters and the Strait of Hormuz closure that paralyzed Gulf exports. Analysts project a 570,000-tonne global deficit, exposing the market to significant upside risk.

item.title: Aluminum Price Analysis - Supply Shocks Drive Market Consolidation
item.description: Aluminum prices remained flat at $3,461.28/Ton globally on April 7, 2026, as markets consolidate following severe supply disruptions. Iranian missile strikes have damaged major smelters in the Emirates, and the Strait of Hormuz closure has paralyzed Gulf exports, with analysts projecting a 570,000-tonne global deficit.

item.title: New Aluminum-Thermal System Unlocks Carbon-Free Industrial Heat and Hydrogen
item.description: A new aluminum-thermal system has been developed that generates carbon-free industrial heat and hydrogen by leveraging aluminum's energy density as a replacement for fossil fuels. This technology represents a significant advancement in decarbonizing industrial processes.

item.title: India's Jindal Steel lifts syngas use on propane crunch
item.description: Indian producer Jindal Steel has begun using synthesis gas (syngas) at its galvanizing and colour coating units to address natural gas and propane shortages. The company previously used syngas for direct-reduced iron (DRI) production. Market turbulence is also influenced by Mideast Gulf war disruptions and US tariff policy changes following a Supreme Court decision.

item.title: Goldman warns downside risk for copper, trims price target
item.description: Goldman Sachs has warned of downside risks for copper prices and trimmed its price target. This reflects concerns over global growth and trade tensions affecting demand. The update comes amid recent volatility in industrial metals.

item.title: Copper Caught Between Trade Tensions and Global Growth
item.description: Copper prices are caught between escalating trade tensions and uncertainty over global growth. This positioning highlights risks to industrial metal demand amid macroeconomic pressures. Market participants await key data like US inflation figures.

item.title: Nickel Price Update: Rose to 17208.75 USD/T on April 8, 2026
item.description: Nickel rose to 17208.75 USD/T on April 8, 2026, up 0.25% from the previous day. Over the past month, prices have fallen 1.27%, but remain 19.34% higher year-over-year. Futures are trading around $17,000 amid structural tightness and shipping disruptions.

item.title: Nickel Futures Rebound to $17,300 per Tonne Amid Middle East Hopes
item.description: Nickel futures rose to around $17,300 per tonne, rebounding from a one-month low, due to hopes for a US-proposed one-month ceasefire with Iran. This boosted risk sentiment and short-term gains in industrial metals. Investor caution persists amid regional tensions.

item.title: Aluminum rose to 3,478 USD/T on April 8, 2026
item.description: Aluminum prices rose to $3,478 per tonne, up 0.39% from the previous day. Over the past month, prices have increased 2.76%, and are up 48.47% compared to the same time last year. Aluminum futures in the UK approached their highest level in more than four years, with market caution driven by geopolitical tensions.

item.title: Iran steel supply unlikely to benefit from ceasefire
item.description: Iran's steel production is unlikely to recover quickly despite a two-week US-Iran ceasefire that may ease energy prices and resume shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Damage to major mills could keep slab and billet prices elevated in Asia and other markets, with fob Asia slab prices up $24/t since late February. The ceasefire has led to sharp drops in European gas and Brent crude prices, potentially lowering production costs, though no immediate impact on steel prices was observed.

item.title: JPMorgan Predicts Aluminium Prices Could Surge Towards $4,000/mt
item.description: JPMorgan forecasts aluminum prices could spike to $4,000 per ton due to supply shocks from damaged GCC and Iranian smelters, which represent 7 mtpa or 10% of global output. Smelter restarts may take 9-12 months, keeping markets tight even if tensions ease. The bank raised its LME forecast from $2,850 to $3,250 per ton amid ongoing disruptions.

item.title: GARMCO Upgrades Hot Rolling Mill for Enhanced Operational Efficiency
item.description: Gulf Aluminium Rolling Mill B.S.C. (GARMCO) completed an upgrade of its 1-stand hot rolling mill through SMS group, enhancing operational efficiency and reliability to meet future demands in aluminum processing.[3]

item.title: Brimstone Reveals 'Fourth Great Refinery' Vision Extending Beyond Carbon-Free Cement
item.description: Brimstone, a U.S. industrial technology innovator, unveiled its Rock Refinery® process roadmap that now targets aluminum, steel, titanium, magnesium, and critical minerals alongside its pioneering carbon-free cement technology.[3]

item.title: Sortera Technologies Plans Second Advanced Scrap Processing Facility
item.description: Sortera Technologies plans to build a second scrap processing facility in Lebanon, Tennessee, using artificial intelligence and advanced sensors to sort mixed aluminum scrap into specific alloys.[3]

item.title: Steel prices, news & market analysis
item.description: The US-Iran ceasefire announcement led to sharp drops in European gas and Brent crude prices, potentially relieving steel production costs, though slab and billet prices in Asia remain elevated due to prior energy and freight cost surges. Traders note no immediate impact on steel prices today, but a stronger euro affects import pricing. Risks persist if the Strait of Hormuz ceasefire is interrupted, deterring Gulf cargoes.

item.title: Plate market fears tightening supplies, escalating freight costs
item.description: US plate market participants report concerns over escalating fuel and freight expenses, tariff complications, long lead times, delivery delays, and shrinking spot availability from domestic mills. Consolidated end-market demand adds pressure. Algoma Steel announced immediate plate price increases of US$60/ton plus a potential fuel surcharge.

item.title: US imposes sanctions on Congo armed group, mining firms over illicit minerals
item.description: The United States announced sanctions against an armed group aligned with illicit mineral activities in Congo and related mining firms. This action targets disruptions in the supply chain for critical minerals including copper. The sanctions aim to curb funding for armed conflicts through mineral trade.

item.title: Aluminum rose to 3,511.25 USD/T on April 10, 2026
item.description: Aluminum prices reached 3,511.25 USD/T on April 10, 2026, up 1.88% from the previous day. The surge is driven by geopolitical tensions, with investors cautious ahead of President Donald Trump's deadline for Iran to agree to a peace deal, amid threats of US strikes on Iranian infrastructure.

item.title: HRC Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: HRC Steel rose to 1,087 USD/T on April 10, 2026, up 0.18% from the previous day, with a 3.43% increase over the past month and 15.88% higher year-over-year. Prices reached 1,086.04 USD/T, up 0.10%, and hit 1,075 USD/T, the highest since January 2024. The data reflects trading on CFDs tracking benchmark markets.

item.title: Copper output from Codelco down nearly 10% in February
item.description: Copper production from Chilean state-run miner Codelco fell 9.8% year-on-year in February to 88,500 metric tons, according to copper commission Cochilco data. Production at BHP’s Escondida mine, the world’s largest copper mine, declined 7.4% to 105,000 tons, while output at Collahuasi rose 56.5% to 26,600 tons.

item.title: Steel Prices, News and Analysis
item.description: US flat steel prices held steady to up on solid demand, ongoing maintenance, and war uncertainty as of April 10. US April domestic scrap prices settled down $20/gt on obsoletes while sideways on primes. Other updates include import BPI price increases in the US, Brazilian iron ore price declines, and new anti-dumping measures in South Africa.

item.title: Peru pulls permit for $1.8B Tia Maria copper mine
item.description: The Peruvian government revoked the permit for the $1.8 billion Tia Maria copper mine project due to legal and technical gaps. This forces a review of the project, which has faced delays for over a decade. The decision impacts planned copper production capacity expansion.

item.title: India’s JSW Steel sees 8% rise in consolidated output in FY 2025-26
item.description: JSW Steel reported an 8% increase in consolidated steel output for the fiscal year 2025-26. This growth reflects strong performance amid favorable market conditions. The announcement highlights ongoing production expansions.

item.title: Tata Steel capacity expansion in India to reach 7 million mt amid strong demand outlook
item.description: Tata Steel plans to expand its capacity in India to 7 million metric tons due to a strong demand outlook. The free article details the strategic move to meet growing market needs. This positions the company for future growth.

item.title: Ukraine reports 5.9 percent increase in pig iron output for Jan-Mar 2026
item.description: Ukraine's pig iron production rose 5.9% in the first quarter of 2026 compared to the prior year. This uptick supports the steel production chain. The report underscores resilience in the sector.

item.title: Nippon Steel starts tests production at new hot rolling line in Nagoya
item.description: Nippon Steel has begun test production on a new hot rolling line at its Nagoya facility. This upgrade enhances production capabilities and efficiency. It marks a key step in capacity improvement.

item.title: Free SAIL’s Bokaro steel mill to start hydrogen injection at BF to cut carbon emissions
item.description: SAIL’s Bokaro steel mill plans to implement hydrogen injection at its blast furnace to reduce carbon emissions. This innovation promotes sustainable steelmaking by integrating hydrogen technology. It represents a step toward lower-emission steel production.
